diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Somatic chest tension is a common feature of acute autonomic panic.
Central crushing chest pressure mandates cardiac exclusion; not achievable via telehealth.
Palpitations with chest pain keep a cardiac cause on the list until in-person assessment.
Hyperventilation is characteristic of an acute panic/autonomic response.

symptoms.symptom_name: Derealisation / feeling 'not really here' with retained insight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Derealisation/depersonalisation with preserved insight is a dissociative trauma response.
Retained insight and absence of hallucination/delusion argue against psychosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset tied to a specific emotional/trauma trigger (documentary about ancestral history)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Context-bound onset to a trauma cue supports a trauma/stressor-related mechanism over spontaneous panic disorder.
Objective sympathetic activation — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers arousal from the patient-reported racing heart/fast breathing, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible anterior chest-wall tenderness on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible tenderness supports a musculoskeletal source.
Reproducible chest-wall tenderness lowers the probability of a cardiac cause — but this is a clinician exam finding the AI cannot obtain, which is precisely why in-person review is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 99% and clear lung fields on auscultation (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal oxygenation and clear auscultation argue against a hypoxic respiratory process — sealed clinician findings not available in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected normal / no acute ischaemic changes, consistent with a non-cardiac cause
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without an ECG, acute coronary syndrome cannot be formally excluded — the reason in-person same-day review is mandated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is the first-line in-person test to exclude a cardiac cause of the chest pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: No current thoughts of self-harm; no hallucinations; insight retained

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Denial of self-harm intent and retained insight lower immediate mental-health-emergency risk — but must be re-screened; a change triggers crisis escalation.
